description:
Split the c11Tests Xcode target into two targets so pure-logic tests can run without launching a c11 DEV.app XCTest host.

# Problem

Today `xcodebuild test -scheme c11-unit` (and `c11-ci`) launches a `c11 DEV.app` as the XCTest host. The host app's main thread is monopolized by serial test execution for ~22 seconds, so its window beachballs and the operator has to Force Quit it after every local test run. CLAUDE.md's Testing policy says "tests must go to CI" as a result; the original framing as "would crash the operator's running c11" turned out to be imprecise — the operator's main c11 is unaffected, it's the freshly-spawned test-host window that freezes (confirmed with Atin 2026-05-15). But the practical pain — no fast local test loop — is real.

# Audit (done 2026-05-15)

101 test files in c11Tests/. Classifier counts grep hits for: `import AppKit`, `import SwiftUI`, `import WebKit`, `NSWindow`, `NSView`, `NSApp`, `MTLCreateSystemDefaultDevice`, `MTKView`, `WKWebView`, `CALayer`, `NSResponder`, `NSEvent`.

- 73 PURE (zero hits) — could run with no AppKit, no host app.
- 28 HOST (1+ hits) — touch UI machinery, need an app host.

That's 72% of the test surface unblockable for fast local iteration. Heavy host-required files by hit count: BrowserPanelTests (163), TerminalAndGhosttyTests (103), BrowserConfigTests (94), CJKIMEInputTests (86), WindowAndDragTests (81), AppDelegateShortcutRoutingTests (61). The pure subset includes all Mailbox tests, most Theme tests, all Workspace snapshot/blueprint/converter tests, all Health parser tests, settings/validation/parser tests, CLI runtime tests.

Full classified file list will be regenerated by the delegator and pinned into the plan doc.

# Approach

1. Add a new Xcode target `c11-logic-tests` (or `c11Tests-Pure`) — a bundle test target with no Host Application, no AppKit dependency. SwiftPM library if simpler.
2. Reclassify each test file's target membership in `GhosttyTabs.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj`:
   - PURE files → new target only.
   - HOST files → stay in existing `c11Tests`.
   - Files that grep flagged on a single innocuous symbol (e.g., an `NSEvent` mention in a comment): re-audit per-file and move if safe.
3. Update schemes:
   - `c11-unit` runs both targets sequentially (matches current behavior).
   - New `c11-logic` scheme runs only the pure target — instant, no app host, agent-runnable locally without operator pain.
4. Update CI workflows (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`, `mailbox-parity.yml`, etc.) to run the new scheme(s).
5. Update CLAUDE.md Testing policy:
   - `xcodebuild test -scheme c11-logic` becomes safe locally.
   - `xcodebuild test -scheme c11-unit` (full + host) still warned against.
   - The "tests must go to CI" rule narrows to "host-required tests must go to CI."

# Risks

- pbxproj surgery is fragile. Hand-edits risk corrupting the project file. Use the `xcodeproj` Ruby gem or a scripted approach. Avoid raw sed.
- A test classified PURE may still call into Sources/ that brings in NSApp transitively. Discover those by compiling the new target and chasing errors.
- 47 pre-existing main test failures (per PR #164's comparison) won't be fixed by this move. Separately tracked.
- Symbol visibility: if c11Tests-pure links a subset of Sources/, some `internal` types may not be visible. Workarounds: `@testable import c11`, or extract a c11Core library.

# Acceptance

- `xcodebuild test -scheme c11-logic` runs and passes (modulo the pre-existing failures that already fail on main).
- The new target runs in <5 seconds (no app launch overhead).
- Local agent test runs no longer spawn a frozen DEV.app window.
- CI runs both targets; total CI time is no worse than today.
- CLAUDE.md Testing policy updated to reflect the new shape.
- PR #164 merged first to avoid pbxproj conflict (the c11mux→c11 rename touches several test files' contents but not their target membership).

# Out of scope

- Fixing the 47 pre-existing main test failures (separate concern; PR #152 covers 3 of them).
- Headless-ifying the host-required target (the "option 1" we considered and dropped — 20+ window-creation sites, partial benefit only).
- Library-extraction refactor (extract c11Core SwiftPM library). Larger; revisit if `@testable import` proves insufficient.

# Provenance

Identified during the c11mux → c11 rename work in PR #164 on 2026-05-15. Atin invested two Force-Quits validating that PR's test run before we realized this was preventing reasonable local validation.
title: Split c11Tests target into pure-logic vs host-required
